name: Midges at the Forest Margin
flavorIntro: The forest margin has been unpassable at dusk for three weeks. A biting, clicking swarm thick enough to turn back a horse. The elves know this ground and can drive them back — if they are fed.
flavorLine: Drive them back before nightfall. They know this ground.

name: Wargs on the Mountain Slopes
region: Ered Luin high passes, above Celondim
flavorIntro: A pack of wargs has come down from the high passes and is ranging the slopes above Celondim. Bold, organized, not ordinary wolves. A well-provisioned company can drive them back.
flavorLine: Not wolves. Wargs. Hearthkeeper food won't hold them.

name: Goblin Scouts at the Forest Margin
flavorIntro: Goblins probing down from the mountain passes above Celondim. They have been watching the tree-line for three nights. They are armored in scavenged plate — not the rabble you expected.
flavorLine: Armored. Organized. Hearthkeeper food will not carry your band through this.

name: Cave Bats in the Lower Halls
region: Thorin's Halls, Blue Mountains — lower tunnels
flavorIntro: The lower access tunnels are impassable. A colony of cave bats, disturbed by recent stonework, has swarmed and blocked the passage. Dense, disorienting, hard to fight in the dark. A fed band can push through.
flavorLine: Tight, dark, and loud. Nothing they haven't handled before.

name: Mountain Wolves on the Outer Passes
region: Blue Mountains, passes above Thorin's Halls
flavorIntro: The outer passes have been dangerous since the last snowmelt. A pack of mountain wolves, lean and territorial, has been raiding supply routes. They move at night and do not yield without a fight.
flavorLine: Mountain wolves. They don't scare easily and they don't leave until they're made to.

name: Goblin Assault on the Outer Gate
flavorIntro: Mountain goblins, mail-clad and organized, hit the outer approach to Thorin's Halls before dawn. Not a raid — a test of the defenses. Your band answers the alarm.
flavorLine: Armored. They know what they're doing. Your band needs to eat before they answer this.

name: Neekerbreekers at the Marsh's Edge
flavorIntro: A hobbit farmer outside Staddle has had enough. The neekerbreekers have been at the marsh-edge crops for three seasons running — a writhing mass of clicking, biting insects. Clear them out, and the farmer will sleep easy.
flavorLine: They know this ground. Should be back by nightfall — if they're fed.

name: Wolves in the Chetwood
flavorIntro: Yellow eyes ring the firelight. The pack has trailed the band since dusk, and hunger has made them bold.
flavorLine: They've been circling since dusk. Hearthkeeper food won't hold them.

name: Goblin Raiders on the East Road
flavorIntro: A goblin warband has come down from the hills and is working the stretch of road east of Weathertop. They have been raiding for days. Your band intercepts them at dusk.
flavorLine: Armored raiders. They've been at this for days. The band needs to be ready.

name: The Wolf-Master of the Chetwood
flavorIntro: The pack is too organized. Someone is running them. A figure at the treeline, and the wolves answer. The Master himself is not large — but the pack does not break until he does.
flavorLine: He runs them like a war-band. Every member needs to be able to take a hit.

stages.label: Wolf-Master
stages.stageFlavor: The pack lands on whoever is most exposed. No one is safe.

name: Rhudaur Men of the East Road
flavorIntro: Hillmen from the east, blood-marked and organized. They have been ranging west along the Road for weeks. Someone is paying them. At their head, a blood-speaker who feeds on the fight itself — his wounds close as yours open.
flavorLine: They do not stop. You have to out-damage whatever is keeping their leader standing.

stages.label: Rhudaur War-band
stages.stageFlavor: The blood-speaker drains your band to refill his own resolve. Out-damage the heal.

name: The Wight of the Barrow-downs
region: Barrow-downs, south of the Great East Road
flavorIntro: The barrow stands alone on the hill. The band has seen it before and turned back — the cold that comes off it is not natural, and the dread that follows is not fear. Come back when you know what you carry into the dark.
flavorLine: Cold and dread. Come back when you are ready for both.

stages.label: Barrow-wight
stages.stageFlavor: Cold seeps through armour. The dread that follows is not fear — it is something older.

name: Dourhand War-band at the Deep Gate
region: Blue Mountains, lower halls of Ered Luin
flavorIntro: Dourhand dwarves, mail-clad and holding the deep gate. They will not yield. They have been here a long time and they know the ground. The armor is the problem — and the clock.
flavorLine: Thick mail and fat resolve. Without penetration you are chipping stone against the clock.

stages.label: Dourhand War-band
stages.stageFlavor: The armor shrugs off most of what you throw. Potency cuts it. Nothing else does.

name: The Drakeling of Ered Luin
flavorIntro: Young, as dragons go — but there is no such thing as a safe dragon. It came for the gold and it found it. The halls it now occupies are impassable. It can be killed, but the fire it breathes does not cool quickly and the scales turn most blades.
flavorLine: Burn it down before the fire burns your band. Potency cuts the scales.

stages.label: Drakeling
stages.stageFlavor: The scales are thick. Without potency, you are chipping stone.

name: The Spider of the Forest Margin
region: Ered Luin, old-growth forest edge above Celondim
flavorIntro: Something large has been taking animals from the forest margin for weeks. Not a wolf. The web-signs are unmistakable to elvish eyes — a spider, old and very large, nested in the deadfall above the tree-line. It picks the softest target. Always.
flavorLine: It hunts the weakest member. Everyone needs to be able to take a hit.

stages.label: Ancient Spider
stages.stageFlavor: It finds the gap in your provisioning. Feed the whole band or lose one of them.

name: The Huorn of the Old Wood
region: Ered Luin, ancient forest above Celondim
flavorIntro: The oldest tree on the ridge has been moving. Not visibly — but the paths shift around it, and the elvish wardens have stopped going near it at dusk. It is not awake, exactly. But it is waking. Kill it before it fully rises.
flavorLine: It starts slow. The longer you take, the worse it becomes.

stages.label: The Huorn
stages.stageFlavor: The roots move. The drain does not stop climbing.
